title: The Legacy of the Split
concept: The split established BERT as the classification default and GPT as the generative default. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Practical NLP Tasks.'

exercises.prompt: Which two default roles emerged from the split of BERT and GPT?

options.text: Both models became search engine filters

exercises.explain: The historic split led to BERT specializing in understanding existing text (classification) and GPT specializing in creating new text (generation).

cards.title: A Tale of Two Paths
cards.body: Have you ever wondered why we use different AI models for different tasks? It all goes back to the historic split when BERT and GPT took two opposite paths!

cards.title: The Legacy of the Split
cards.body: The legacy of the split refers to how BERT and GPT became the gold standards for different tasks. BERT became the default for understanding and organizing text (classification), while GPT became the default for creating new text (generation).

cards.title: The Two Specialists
cards.body: Imagine you are running a restaurant. You hire BERT as your quality control inspector who reads every ingredient carefully to make sure the food is safe. You hire GPT as your creative chef who makes up brand new recipes from scratch. They both started with the same basic training, but they went on to do completely different, specialized jobs.

cards.title: Real-World Roles
cards.body: When you use search engines, BERT is often working behind the scenes to understand exactly what you mean. When you ask an AI chatbot to write a custom story or poem for you, GPT is the one doing the creative writing.

cards.title: Is One Model Better?
cards.body: Some people think that one model is simply 'better' than the other. In reality, they are just different! BERT is built to analyze what already exists, while GPT is built to invent what doesn't exist yet. Both are champion models in their own leagues.

cards.title: Dividing the Labor
cards.body: Let's compare the two standard roles established by this split.

right.text: Predicts the next word to create, write, and invent new text from scratch.

left.text: Reads all directions at once to analyze, sort, and understand existing text.

exercises.prompt: What became BERT's primary specialty after the split?
exercises.explain: BERT reads text in both directions to understand context, making it the default choice for analyzing and classifying existing text.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The split established BERT as the classification default and GPT as the generative default.
exercises.explain: Yes! The split created two distinct defaults: BERT for reading/analyzing (classification) and GPT for writing/creating (generation).

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of BERT's main strength.
exercises.sentence: BERT is the default model for text understanding, while GPT is the default for generation.
exercises.explain: BERT reads text bidirectionally (in both directions) to gain a deep understanding of context, making it perfect for classifying and analyzing data.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is likely powered by a generative GPT-style model rather than a BERT-style model?

options.text: An AI that reads customer emails and automatically tags them as 'Urgent' or 'Spam'
An AI assistant that writes a personalized birthday poem for your friend
An AI search engine that highlights the best answer on a webpage

exercises.explain: The second system is likely GPT because it is dynamically creating a new response (generative), whereas classifying spam is an understanding task best suited for BERT.

exercises.prompt: How did the split permanently shape the roles of BERT and GPT in AI?

options.text: BERT was retired and completely replaced by GPT.
Both models are now used only for generating new text.
BERT became the standard for understanding tasks, while GPT became the standard for generating text.
The split proved that reading text and writing text require the exact same model setup.

exercises.explain: BERT became the king of understanding (classification), while GPT became the master of creating (generation). Together, they define modern NLP. Next up: Practical NLP Tasks.
